title: Astronaut
description: Astronauts are highly trained professionals who travel into space to conduct scientific research, operate spacecraft, and contribute to space exploration missions.
content: Astronauts undergo rigorous physical, mental, and academic training. They often begin their careers as scientists, engineers, or military pilots before being selected by space agencies like ISRO or NASA. This career requires strong problem-solving skills, discipline, and the ability to operate in extreme conditions.

financialFeasibility.note: Extensive higher education and competitive entrance exams make this path more accessible to students from well-resourced backgrounds. Scholarships are available but rare.

sectorScore.remarks: Government space agencies (e.g., ISRO, NASA) are the primary employers. Private sector access is growing but still limited.

relatedCareers.reason: Designs spacecraft and navigation systems for space missions.
relatedCareers.title: Aerospace Engineer

relatedCareers.reason: Conducts scientific research related to space and celestial phenomena.
relatedCareers.title: Astrophysicist

relatedCareers.reason: Shares foundational skills like navigation, simulation, and spatial awareness.
relatedCareers.title: Pilot

genderDistribution.note: Historically male-dominated, but increasing efforts to promote female participation in STEM and space programs are gradually shifting the balance.
